tagline: A running lab notebook of Claude experiments — model head-to-heads, effort sweeps, and the routing decisions they produce. Every number here comes from a measured run, not a guess.

items.title: Opus 4.8 vs GPT 5.6 Sol
items.subtitle: Both signed AAB artifacts complete
items.blurb: Both contenders produced real signed Flutter Android App Bundles. GPT leads the preliminary objective artifact audit 96-91. Build-phase new tokens were ~432,615 (Opus) vs 285,373 (GPT) across two different harnesses — the same order of magnitude, so no clean token-efficiency winner is claimed.

items.title: Fable 5 vs Opus 4.8
items.subtitle: building BurnZone via Kriya
items.blurb: Is the most capable model worth 2× to build a real brownfield? On the two build/design tasks it ran, Fable 5 tied Opus 4.8 at ~2× the cost — and on both security tasks its classifiers declined and Claude Code silently served Opus instead.

items.title: Sonnet 5 vs Opus 4.8
items.subtitle: ultrathink · max effort
items.blurb: Does cranking to max effort change the verdict? Recall rose ~1.7x the cost, gate decisions didn't move, and one blind spot survived.

items.title: Sonnet 5 vs Opus 4.8
items.subtitle: default effort
items.blurb: The first head-to-head on a real Apolaki build under Kriya. Headline: cheaper per token != cheaper per task.

items.title: The Fable Field Guide
items.subtitle: explainer
items.blurb: How to actually use Claude Fable 5 well — the quirks, the pitfalls, and copy-paste patterns.
